obligations.obligation:
(“Petitioner”/ “NTPC”), has filed the present Petition
under Section 79 (1) (a) & (k) read with Section 62 of the Electricity Act, 2003 (“the

obligations.obligation:
The Petitioner is a Government Company within the meaning of the
Companies Act, 1956 and a ‘Generating Company’ as defined under Section 2(28) of
the Electricity Act, 2003 (the Act).

obligations.obligation:
Subsequently, the Petitioner filed Petition No. 294/GT/2020 dated 30.10.2019
for truing-up of 2014-19 tariff under Regulation 8(1) of the 2014 Tariff Regulations.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
In
its additional submissions dated 10.5.2022 in the said Petition, the Petitioner claimed
Rs. 454.49 lakh towards procurement of 10 wagons under Regulation 14(3)(x) of the
2014 Tariff Regulations.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
Accordingly, since the issue of wagon capitalization was pending adjudication
before the APTEL, the Commission, vide order dated 19.5.2024 in Petition No.
294/GT/2020 (read with corrigendum dated 15.6.2024), deferred consideration of the
claim while granting liberty to the Petitioner to approach the...
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
Order in Petition No.424/MP/2025 Page 4 of 20(b) Regulation 14(3)(x) of the 2014 Tariff Regulations provides for capitalization of
expenditure necessitated on account of modifications in the fuel receiving
system arising due to circumstances not within the control of the generating
station.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
This expenditure is, therefore, fully justified under the
provisions of Regulation 14(3)(x) of the 2014 Tariff Regulations.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
The principles laid down in the APTEL’s judgment
must therefore be applied to the present case.
obligations.short_summary: Application of APTEL's judgment principles to current case

obligations.obligation:
The
Petitioner must still satisfy Regulation 14(3)(x) of the 2014 Tariff Regulations
through a station-specific prudence check on necessity and control-beyond
circumstances.
obligations.short_summary: Station-specific prudence check required for Regulation compliance.

obligations.obligation:
(b) Regulation 14(3)(x) of the 2014 Tariff Regulations permits additional
capitalization only where the same is necessitated on account of
modifications in the fuel receiving system arising from non-materialisation of
coal linkage or other circumstances not within the control of the generating
stati...
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
(d) Further, the Petitioner must demonstrate proper decapitalization of old and
unserviceable wagons through detailed accounting records, so as to avoid
double-counting of assets and impermissible duplication of cost recovery.
obligations.risk_level: Medium to High depending on current asset management practices and historical compliance record
obligations.due_date: 2023-12-31 or within six months from the date of filing petition, N/A for ongoing processes
obligations.short_summary: Ensure accurate decapitalization of old wagons by maintaining detailed accounting records.

obligations.obligation:
While wagons may be essential to the operation of the generating station,
their characterisation as non-minor assets does not, by itself, entitle the
Petitioner to capitalization, such claims must still withstand the prudence
check and can only be admitted upon clear demonstration of exceptional
cir...
obligations.short_summary: Wagon capitalization requires exceptional circumstances under Tariff Regulations.

obligations.obligation:
The subsequent APTEL judgment
dated 22.11.2024 in the said Appeal has conclusively upheld capitalization
of wagons under Regulation 14(3)(x) of the 2014 Tariff Regulations.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
Hence, the present
Petition squarely falls within the protective ambit of the Commission’s order
and Regulation 14(3)(x) of the 2014 Tariff Regulations.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
Regulation 14(3)(x) of
the 2014 Tariff Regulations does not restrict additional capitalization only to
situations of complete failure of linkage.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
(f) BSPHCL’s reliance on the bare wording of Regulation 14(3)(x) of the
2014 Tariff Regulations to argue that NTPC has not proved uncontrollable
exigencies ignores the binding precedent of the Hon’ble Supreme Court and
the APTEL.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
In view of these facts, the
requirement of prudence check under Regulation 14(3)(x) of the 2014 Tariff
Regulations stands duly met.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
NTPC has
categorically clarified in its Petition that the wagons are capital assets of
significant operational importance and not minor assets under the proviso to
Regulation 14(3)(x) of the 2014 Tariff Regulations.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
The
expenditure of Rs. 454.49 lakh towards procurement of 10 wagons for
Kahalgaon STPS-I, necessitated due to operational exigencies and
replacement of unserviceable wagons, fully satisfies the requirements of
Regulation 14(3)(x) of the 2014 Tariff Regulation.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
The issue for our
consideration is whether the additional capital expenditure of Rs. 454.49 lakh incurred
by the Petitioner towards the procurement of 10 railway wagons for Kahalgaon STPS
Stage-I during FY 2014-15 is admissible under Regulation 14(3)(x) of the 2014 Tariff
Regulations.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
In view of the decision of the APTEL`s judgment dated 22.11.2024 and in terms
of Regulation 14(3)(x) of the 2014 Tariff Regulations, the additional capital expenditure
of Rs. 454.49 lakh incurred towards procurement of 10 railway wagons for Kahalgaon
STPS Stage-I in FY 2014-15 is admissible.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)
